title: Marks and Jumps
brief: Set a mark, jump to another line, and jump back to patch metadata.
objectives: Set value to done in one place.
Update owner line near the top.
Use mark/jump style workflow and save.
hints: Set mark on owner line: ma.
Jump to end with G and fix value token.
Jump back with `a and fix owner, save, then ./stage check.
primary_path: Place mark `a` on owner line with `ma`.
Jump to bottom using `G`, set `value: done`, then jump back with `` `a ``.
Set `owner: nvim-team`, save with `:w`, then run `./stage check`.
fallback_path: Jump list fallback: `<C-o>` to go back and `<C-i>` to go forward.
Use `/owner` and `/value` searches if mark placement is missed.
why_path: Marks let you bounce between distant edit points without rescanning the file.
pro_move: Use marks for two-point edits, especially in longer files.
